frame_analyses.description: Equipment setup and assembly phase. Showing FLS trainer module with pegs, adjustment balls, endoscopic camera, and connector cable setup.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructional demonstration of proper equipment assembly. Camera adjustment and focus calibration shown.

frame_analyses.description: Camera rod being plugged into connector. Showing proper camera positioning and focus adjustment technique.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates correct camera setup procedure with 360° adjustment ball and focus calibration for endoscopic view.

frame_analyses.description: Module being fixed to work surface with screws and white caps. Showing proper stabilization technique.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates fixation best practice - using white cap screws, noting that 2-3 screws are typically sufficient for stability.

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic camera view of peg transfer module displayed on monitor. Showing two bright green rubber objects on pegs, blue heart-shaped foam objects, and pink/red handles visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear endoscopic visualization. Camera properly focused and positioned for task execution. Module layout clearly visible.

frame_analyses.description: Text overlay reads 'training demonstration'. Single laparoscopic instrument visible in field. Green objects positioned on left peg column, blue heart objects in center area.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Beginning of actual peg transfer demonstration. Shows expert-level hand positioning and instrument control.

frame_analyses.description: Black laparoscopic grasper picking up bright green rubber object from left peg. Smooth grasping motion visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Confident, controlled grasp of peg object. Proper grasper positioning at mid-shaft of object. Smooth instrument control.

frame_analyses.description: Green object being transferred through midair space between pegs. Two instruments visible - one holding green object, other prepared for receiving position.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth transfer movement. Minimal wasted motion. Expert-level coordination between two instruments visible.

frame_analyses.description: Green object being placed onto right side peg using dominant hand grasper. Clean placement motion.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Precise placement directly onto peg. No fumbling or repositioning required. Demonstrates expert technique.

frame_analyses.description: Reverse phase beginning. Green object from right peg being grasped and lifted by dominant hand instrument.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean pickup demonstrating reverse direction transfer. Symmetric movement pattern to forward phase.

frame_analyses.description: Green object in transit back toward left pegs. Both instruments working in coordination for smooth transfer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Efficient midair movement. Minimal hesitation. Direct path between pegs.

frame_analyses.description: Green object being placed back onto left peg completing the transfer cycle. Clean final positioning.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Precise placement completing the transfer sequence. Demonstrates expert-level object manipulation.

frame_analyses.description: Overview showing multiple transferred objects. Text indicates reference to FLS video for additional details.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstration showing multiple successful peg transfers. Module showing various colored objects in proper end positions.

task_specific_assessments.video_type_note: This is an instructional/tutorial video demonstrating proper FLS peg transfer technique. No actual trainee performance is being scored.
task_specific_assessments.demonstrated_objects_transferred: At least 2-3 bright green rubber objects clearly shown transferred between pegs
task_specific_assessments.instrument_control: Expert-level smooth, coordinated movements with minimal wasted motion
task_specific_assessments.error_avoidance: No dropped objects, fumbling, or improper placements shown in demonstration

score_components.formula_applied: Not applicable - video is instructional demonstration, not trainee performance

confidence_rationale: Video classification is definitively instructional based on: (1) Equipment setup and assembly tutorial at beginning, (2) Text overlays reading 'training demonstration' and instructional notes, (3) Narration stating 'The training is similar with peg transfer' and 'More details can reference the FLS video in U disc', (4) No timer or formal FLS assessment environment, (5) Focus on teaching proper technique rather than scoring performance. All observable elements are clear and well-framed.
cannot_determine: Exact completion time - video shows abbreviated demonstration, not full timed performance
Full object count - only 2-3 transfers clearly visible in demonstration portion
Official FLS score - this is instructional content, not a testable performance
technique_summary: This is an educational tutorial demonstrating expert-level peg transfer technique. The video includes proper equipment assembly, camera setup, calibration procedures, and smooth, efficient object transfers. No errors or penalties are present in the demonstrated technique. The content is designed to teach proper FLS peg transfer methodology.
strengths: Clear instructional setup showing equipment assembly step-by-step
Expert-level smooth instrument coordination in peg transfers
No dropped objects or improper placements in demonstrated transfers
Efficient movements with minimal wasted motion
Multiple colored objects used in demonstration for clarity

demonstrated_techniques: Proper FLS trainer module assembly and fixation
Camera focus and 360° adjustment ball calibration
Module stabilization using white cap screws (2-3 sufficient)
Proper bimanual grasper coordination for object pickup
Efficient midair object transfer between pegs
Precise object placement on destination peg
Smooth hand coordination and instrument exchange
verbal_cues: Narrator indicates 'The training is similar with peg transfer'
References to FLS video in U disc for additional details
Equipment assembly guidance: 'It has 4 screws with white cap, normally 2 or 3 is enough'
Camera adjustment instruction: 'Adjust the camera rod for a correct image'
Focus adjustment: 'Adjust the focus for clear image'
common_errors_addressed: Improper camera positioning - shows correct adjustment technique
Insufficient module fixation - demonstrates proper screw installation
Blurred endoscopic view - shows focus calibration method
Dropped objects - not demonstrated; expert technique avoids this
Fumbling or imprecise placement - demonstration shows smooth, direct movements

improvement_suggestions: This is instructional content demonstrating correct technique - not applicable for improvement suggestions
Video effectively teaches proper FLS equipment setup and peg transfer execution
